1. Nike Inc. (NKE)

Apparel & Retail · $51bn

Large Enterprise (>$3B) Apparel & Retail

HIGH
 

Nike Stock Plunges 30% in 2026 on China Sales Warning and Supply Chain Disruptions

TradingKey

KPIs — FY2026 (TTM ending Feb 2026)

Nike Q3 FY2026 earnings released March 31, 2026

Inv days122d▼
Gross margin40.2%-130 bps
Inv value$7.5bn
  

Key Issues

Greater China sales collapse

20% Q4 sales decline forecast

Management warned China sales will drop 20% in Q4 FY2026 due to inventory cleanup and marketplace rebalancing amid local brand competition.

Capabilities Needed

Demand sensing and forecastingRegional inventory optimizationMarket-driven replenishment

Gross margin compression from tariffs

300 bps North America tariff impact

Tariffs created 300 bps margin headwind in North America in Q3, with management expecting 250 bps impact continuing into Q4.

Capabilities Needed

Sourcing optimizationLanded cost modelingSupplier diversification planning

EMEA inventory buildup geopolitical disruption

Middle East conflict supply chain impact

CFO warned of unplanned volatility from Middle East disruption and rising oil prices affecting input costs and consumer behavior.

Capabilities Needed

Risk scenario planningMulti-echelon inventory optimizationAgile supply network design
WC: $600m-$900mInv: -8%Margin: +200bps

Where the value lies

Nike holds $7.5bn inventory with 122 days on hand while gross margin compressed 130 bps to 40.2% in Q3. Optimizing regional inventory placement, especially clearing EMEA buildup and China excess, combined with tariff-mitigated sourcing strategies, could release $600-900m working capital and restore 200 bps margin over 18 months.

Accenture Solutions

Planning Opportunities

Implement integrated S&OP across geographies with regional demand sensing to prevent inventory mismatches between North America growth and China/EMEA declines.

Target Operating Model

Platform Opportunities

o9 Solutions provides real-time planning across multi-regional supply networks with embedded scenario modeling for tariff impacts and geopolitical disruptions.

Planning Platform Reinvention

Supplier Integration PlanningSupply Chain SynchronizationCost-to-Serve Analysis

~25 jets requiring rework; 10 unit Q1 delay impact

Capabilities Needed

Quality Planning & ComplianceProduction SchedulingDemand-Supply Matching

Widebody delivery volume below target

787 stabilized at 8/month vs 10/month plan

787 production at 8/month with supply chain seat certification delays limiting delivery ramp despite improved rework processes.

Capabilities Needed

Supplier Capacity PlanningProduction Flow OptimizationConstraint-Based Planning
WC: $1800mInv: -12%Margin: +750bps

Where the value lies

Boeing's $682bn backlog and inventory liquidation path to positive cash flow ($1-3bn FCF guidance 2026) offers $1.8bn working capital opportunity by accelerating 737/787 delivery conversion through integrated Spirit planning. Margin recovery from -8% to break-even by Q4 2026 represents 750-800bps improvement contingent on production rate stability, quality escape reduction, and supplier synchronization across 15,000 newly integrated Spirit employees.

Accenture Solutions

Planning Opportunities

Integrated S&OP to synchronize Spirit's Wichita, Dallas, Tulsa, Belfast facilities with Boeing's 737/787/777X production, reduce quality escapes, and accelerate inventory-to-delivery conversion across $47bn debt-laden balance sheet.

Target Operating Model

Platform Opportunities

Cyberattack paralyzed online operations for 7 weeks

£393m lost profit

April 2025 Scattered Spider ransomware attack disrupted online clothing orders, click-and-collect, and inventory systems through July 2025.

Capabilities Needed

Cyber-Resilient Supply Chain PlanningReal-Time Inventory VisibilityOmnichannel Fulfillment Orchestration

Inventory disruption and manual stock allocation

£101.6m direct incident costs

Systems outage forced pen-and-paper inventory tracking causing food markdown, wastage, and delivery failures still reported May 2026.

Capabilities Needed

Automated Demand-Supply MatchingDistribution Requirements Planning (DRP)S&OP Process Automation

Fashion/Home/Beauty margin compression from excess seasonal stock

£213.4m operating profit (down from £478m)

Markdown and clearance of excess seasonal stock accumulated during outage, with Fashion/Home/Beauty adjusted profit down 55%.

Capabilities Needed

Markdown OptimizationSeasonal Product Lifecycle PlanningAssortment Planning
WC: $250mInv: -12%Margin: +150bps

Where the value lies

M&S faces £393m profit loss from cyberattack plus ongoing delivery delays and inventory disruption. With £157m supply chain automation investment committed over 3 years and gross margin at 38.8% (up 0.6%), opportunity exists to recover 150-200bps margin through enhanced planning capabilities, reduce inventory 10-15%, and prevent future disruption costs exceeding £100m.

Accenture Solutions

Planning Opportunities

Rebuild S&OP and IBP processes with cyber-resilient, real-time planning across demand-supply-inventory-fulfillment to prevent future £393m disruptions and enable £157m automation ROI.

Gross margin43.5%+2.0%
Inv value$1.68bn

Erfurt closure restructuring charges 2,700 jobs

€150m-200m

Closing legacy fulfillment center by Sept 2026 following About You acquisition creates excess capacity and drives restructuring costs.

Capabilities Needed

Network optimization modelingCapacity planningScenario planning

About You integration margin compression

-70bps

Q1 2026 net income dropped to -€87.6m from +€9.9m in Q1 2025 due to integration restructuring costs despite gross margin stability.

Merger integration planningDemand-supply balancingCost-to-serve modeling

Inventory growth outpacing revenue 7.5% vs 4.2%

$126m

Inventory rose to $1.68bn (+7.5%) while revenue grew only 4.2%, signaling overstocking and working capital strain from dual-brand consolidation.

Inventory optimizationDemand forecastingSKU rationalization
WC: $90m-120mInv: -5.5%Margin: +80bps

Zalando faces immediate working capital release opportunity of $90-120m by aligning inventory growth with revenue trajectory, reducing excess stock accumulated from About You integration. With network consolidation to 14 fulfillment centers and €210m capex in automation, optimizing demand-supply planning can unlock 80-100bps margin improvement while absorbing restructuring charges through efficiency gains.

1. Nexperia Netherlands

Electronics & High Tech · $2.06bn

Large Enterprise (>$3B) Electronics & High Tech

HIGH
 

Nexperia crisis triggers global semiconductor supply chain disruptions affecting automakers

Automotive Logistics Media

KPIs — FY2024

Nexperia press release, May 2025

Limited public data
  

Key Issues

Government seizure halted China export flows

$1.2bn revenue exposure (60% automotive)

Dutch government seized control October 2025; China blocked exports from assembly facilities where 80% of products processed.

Capabilities Needed

Geopolitical Risk PlanningMulti-Echelon Inventory OptimizationSupply Network RedesignDual-Source Strategy Planning

Automotive production stops across OEM customers

Honda cut 110,000 units, $969m loss

VW, Mercedes, Stellantis face Q1 2026 shutdowns as existing inventories last 2-4 weeks only.

Capabilities Needed

Demand Sensing & ResponseAllocation PlanningSupplier Collaboration PlatformProduction Recovery Planning

40% market share in critical transistors

6-month qualification delay alternative suppliers

Nexperia controls 40% of discrete semiconductor market; automakers cannot quickly re-qualify alternative chip suppliers.

Capabilities Needed

Scenario Planning & SimulationMulti-Source Qualification PlanningRisk-Adjusted Supply PlanningLong-Term Capacity Planning
WC: $180mInv: -12%Margin: +150bps

Where the value lies

With $2.06bn revenue and improving gross margins in Q1 2025, Nexperia needs to rebuild supply network resilience and optimize inventory positioning across geopolitically-separated zones (Netherlands vs China). Working capital opportunity of $180m exists through optimized inventory pre-positioning and dual-region buffer strategies, while 150bps margin improvement achievable through reduced expedite costs and allocation optimization as supply stabilizes through 2026.

Accenture Solutions

Planning Opportunities

Implement integrated S&OP with geopolitical risk layers to balance European and Asian supply chains with demand allocation across automotive OEMs.

Target Operating Model

Platform Opportunities

o9 Solutions enables real-time multi-echelon inventory optimization, what-if scenario planning for geopolitical disruptions, and constraint-based allocation across fragmented supply networks.

Planning Platform Reinvention

👥 Similar work: TDK, Olympus

Planning as a Service

Rapid deployment of Accenture-managed planning control tower to stabilize operations during governance transition and legal proceedings through end of 2026.

AI & Analytics

Supply Risk Monitor + Allocation Optimizer

AI agents monitor 100m+ geopolitical data sources for early warning signals and optimize real-time allocation across OEM customers facing production volatility.

AI & Analytics

Confidence: high

Gross margin45.2%+80bps
  

Key Issues

Tariff impact across all segments

€250-300m annualized

Tariffs detracted 320 basis points from Q1 2026 margin with ongoing pressure throughout 2026 requiring aggressive supply chain regionalization.

Capabilities Needed

Multi-echelon network optimizationScenario planningSupplier collaboration

Supply chain regionalization urgency

€250m mitigation target by 2028

Management accelerating shift to local-for-local production across US, Europe, and Asia to reduce tariff exposure by end of 2028.

Capabilities Needed

Network designDual sourcing strategyRisk modeling

Margin compression despite growth

-320bps from tariffs/inflation

Q1 2026 achieved only 40bps net margin expansion despite 4% comparable sales growth as cost inflation and tariffs offset 260bps of productivity gains.

Capabilities Needed

Cost-to-serve analyticsMargin optimizationPrice-cost modeling
WC: $200mInv: -12%Margin: +150bps

Where the value lies

With €17.8bn revenue and 45% gross margins facing 320bps tariff/inflation headwinds, optimizing supply chain network design and supplier mix could unlock €150-200m in margin recovery beyond the €815m productivity savings already delivered. Reducing inventory across regionalized networks while improving OTIF in the transition to local-for-local manufacturing offers additional €200m+ working capital release given scale of operations across 46 US sites and global footprint.

Demand sensingInventory optimizationMulti-echelon planning

Supply chain complexity and delays

160bps margin opportunity

Markdown costs remain elevated

$280m annual markdown cost

Despite slight improvement, markdown costs remain high requiring promotional activities due to cautious consumers and inventory composition challenges.

Capabilities Needed

Assortment planningPrice optimizationS&OP integration
WC: $640mInv: -17%Margin: +200bps

Where the value lies

H&M can unlock $640m in working capital by reducing inventory from 15.6% to Inditex benchmark of 13% of sales while achieving additional 200bps gross margin improvement through better demand planning and markdown reduction. Current inventory productivity at 10-year high presents platform to drive toward normalized 54-55% gross margin target through enhanced S&OP and supplier collaboration.

Demand sensing regionalPortfolio mix optimizationChannel planning
WC: $800mInv: -8%Margin: +150bps

Integrated business planningCost-to-serve modelingPrice and promotion optimization

EPS miss vs forecast

Reverse Logistics AutomationAI-Powered TriageRobotics Deployment

4% waste rate

Capabilities Needed

Workforce Optimization AIHumanoid RoboticsPredictive Labor Planning

Where the value lies

GXO can unlock $800M in labor savings by scaling automation from 30% to 60% penetration across 970+ facilities while reducing returns processing from 96 hours to 48 hours. Deploying autonomous systems and AI-driven reverse logistics can improve returns-to-stock rates from 96% to 98%, recovering $200M+ in annual inventory value.

🏭 Autonomous Warehousing

Deploy Blue Yonder WMS with AI-powered labor planning and slotting optimization across 970+ sites to reduce cost-to-serve and improve returns processing velocity.

🚚 Autonomous Transportation

📦 Autonomous Order Mgmt

Deploy omnichannel OMS to enable real-time returns visibility, automated RMA workflows, and instant refund triggers for 40+ customer geographies.

Quality AnalyticsReturns PreventionInventory Disposition

Where the value lies

Amazon's $98bn fulfillment cost base at 33% of revenue and mandatory seller returns compliance create $15-20bn optimization opportunity through autonomous warehouse systems, reverse logistics automation, and AI-driven returns prevention. 🏭 Autonomous Warehousing

Manhattan WMS with robotics integration can reduce Amazon's 33% fulfillment cost ratio through autonomous putaway, dynamic slotting for 1.575M employee workforce, and returns disposition automation.

Blue Yonder TMS can optimize AMZL last-mile network costs and manage 3.5% fuel surcharge impact across multi-carrier network with dynamic routing.

📦 Autonomous Order Mgmt

🤖 Agentic AI & Digital Twin

🤖 Agentic AI & Digital Twin

AI agents simulating 12M frozen case flows and 385-store annual expansion can predict throughput bottlenecks and optimize cold-chain slotting before Americold contract renewal.
